10.reason: [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers due to removal from C standard]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers due to removal from C standard]

38.reason: [Loop runs to i <= strlen(b), which accesses b[strlen(b)] 鈥 an out-of-bounds null terminator, causing undefined behavior or wrong output]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop runs to i <= strlen(b), which accesses b[strlen(b)] 鈥 an out-of-bounds null terminator, causing undefined behavior or wrong output]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This loop runs from i=1 to i<=strlen(b), which accesses b[strlen(b)] 鈥 an out-of-bounds index since valid indices are 0 to strlen(b)-1. This causes undefined behavior, but more critically, if b is empty (e.g., input "0.0"), b[0] access above is also invalid. However, the immediate compilation error is due to 'gets'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop runs to i == strlen(b), which accesses b[strlen(b)] 鈥 an out-of-bounds null terminator, causing undefined behavior or wrong output]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop runs to i <= strlen(b), which accesses b[strlen(b)] 鈥 an out-of-bounds index since valid indices are 0 to strlen(b)-1; this leads to undefined behavior, but not a compilation error. However, the primary compilation error is due to 'gets']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop runs to i == strlen(b), which accesses b[strlen(b)] 鈥 an out-of-bounds null terminator, causing undefined behavior or wrong output]
